metadata.strategy: Steady passive income - high confidence, diversified
metadata.resolution_notes: All NO positions won. BTC range $89.2k-$93.7k, ETH range $3.1k-$3.3k, SOL range $126-$143. Rebalance on Jan 7 flipped YES→NO on w4/w5, sold w3/w6.

positions.reasoning: BTC at ~$91k needs 10% rally in 6 days - unlikely without major catalyst
positions.resolution_notes: BTC peaked at $93,739 on Jan 7, never reached $100k. NO wins.

positions.reasoning: ETH at ~$3,300, would need 10% crash - strong support above $3k
positions.resolution_notes: ETH low ~$3,100 per Binance 1-min candles. Did not reach $3,000. NO wins.

positions.reasoning: BTC support at $90k, $88k breach would require significant selling
positions.resolution_notes: Sold on Jan 8 at entry price. BTC low was $89,234, $88k NOT reached. Would have won.

positions.reasoning: Flipped from YES to NO on Jan 7. Already deep in the money.
positions.resolution_notes: ETH never hit $3,400 (started ~$3,300, dropped to ~$3,100). NO wins. Originally YES, flipped Jan 7.

positions.reasoning: Flipped from YES to NO on Jan 7. Already deep in the money.
positions.resolution_notes: BTC never hit $96k (peaked $93,739 on Jan 7). NO wins. Originally YES, flipped Jan 7.

positions.reasoning: SOL at ~$139, needs 8% rally - post-crash recovery slower than expected
positions.resolution_notes: Sold on Jan 8 at entry price. SOL max ~$143, $150 NOT hit. Would have won.

summary.notes: 4/4 remaining positions won at resolution. Rebalance on Jan 7 (flipping YES→NO) caused -$168.32 realized loss. Without the rebalance, the original YES positions would have lost -$169.60 anyway.

daily_tracking.notes: Unrealized +$2.68. WARNING: ETH $3k dip risk increased to 31%. Sold w3, w6.
RESOLUTION: All 4 remaining NO positions won. Payout $379.00. w1 +$14.95, w2 +$15.20, w4 +$0.36, w5 +$0.32.

grace_hopper_calculus.lesson: Rebalance was correct directionally (all NO positions won) but the cost of flipping YES→NO at near-zero was devastating. Better to cut YES positions earlier before they went to near-zero, or accept the smaller loss from holding YES to resolution.
